jobText:
About the job
POSITION DETAILS

Brooklyn College is soliciting applications for an Administrative Coordinator who will coordinate activities and ticket operations with the highest degree of customer service and accuracy for the Tow Performing Arts Center Box Office. Reporting to the Associate Bursar, the Box Office Coordinator will respond promptly to customer inquiries and requests, and train Box Office personnel and collaborate with Tow Center personnel.

In addition to the CUNY Title Overview, typical duties include but are not limited to:

Process ticket sales in-person, over the phone, and online, including handling cash, credit card transactions, and processing vouchers or exchanges.
Provide information on showtimes, ticket availability, and seating (including ADA requirements).
Sort and distribute tickets to patrons, internal departments and external companies.
Respond to customer inquiries and problems via email, window, day of event, or phone.
Responsible for all event creation and maintenance of ShoWare Ticketing System
Reconcile daily cash drawers, Box Office receipts and event income.
Responsible for secure transfer on all monies taken in, deposited into secure combination safe and bought to Student Payment Services (Office of the Bursar) for deposit according to CUNY cash accountability policy.
Coordinate with internal departments and external companies regarding operating procedure to maximize operational efficiency.
Develop training materials and procedural manuals and implement usage of same.
Provide sales/revenue reports to supervisor and colleagues.
Supervise and train office staff.
Other duties as assigned.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor's Degree required.

Preferred Qualifications

Strong communication, customer service, mathematical, analytical, and computer skills
Excellent time management and organizational skills
Flexibility to work weekends, evenings, and holidays as needed
Knowledge of ShoWare ticketing system or similar program
Experience in ticket sales and distribution

Cuny Title Overview

Supports projects, initiatives, and activities that impact an academic or administrative department.

Manages basic office functions such as communications, meeting schedules, work plans, staff assignments, and distributing information.
Collects data for, prepares, and distributes reports and presentations using word processing, spreadsheet, and presentation software.
Updates office databases, lists, and files to ensure completeness and accuracy; maintains current information on department web site(s); maintains department archives and collections.
Conducts internet and/or database research and performs basic systems queries to locate information related to department activities.
Maintains department fiscal plans and budgets; assists in budget administration and invoice processing.
Provides basic information, instructions, and materials as requested by students, faculty, and others who contact the department.
May supervise office staff and student workers.
Performs related duties as assigned.
